question: What are the top 5 products by revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5;

question: What is the average order value?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(total_amount) AS avg_order_value FROM orders;

question: How many orders were placed in each state?
expected_sql: SELECT c.state, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.state ORDER BY order_count DESC;

question: Which customers have never placed an order?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL;

question: What is total revenue by month?
expected_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', order_date) AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders GROUP BY month ORDER BY month;

question: Which products have never been purchased?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name FROM products p LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = p.product_id WHERE oi.order_item_id IS NULL;

question: Who are the 10 most valuable customers by total spend?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_spend FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_id ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10;

question: What is the average quantity per order item?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(quantity) AS avg_quantity FROM order_items;

question: How many customers are there in total?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customers;

question: How many products are in each category?
expected_sql: SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY product_count DESC;

question: What is the total revenue generated by each product category?
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY revenue DESC;

question: What is the most expensive product?
expected_sql: SELECT product_name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 1;

question: How many orders were placed in 2024?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE strftime('%Y', order_date) = '2024';

question: What is the total number of items sold?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(quantity) AS total_units FROM order_items;

question: Which 5 customers placed the most orders?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 5;

question: What is the average product price by category?
expected_sql: SELECT category, AVG(price) AS avg_price FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY avg_price DESC;

question: How many orders does each customer have on average?
expected_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS avg_orders_per_customer FROM orders;

question: What are the top 10 best selling products by units sold?
expected_sql: SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS units_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id ORDER BY units_sold DESC LIMIT 10;

question: How many customers signed up in each year?
expected_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y', signup_date) AS year, COUNT(*) AS signups FROM customers GROUP BY year ORDER BY year;

question: What is the total revenue for the Electronics category?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.product_id = oi.product_id WHERE p.category = 'Electronics';

question: Which state has the most customers?
expected_sql: SELECT state, COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY state ORDER BY customer_count DESC LIMIT 1;

question: What is the highest single order total?
expected_sql: SELECT MAX(total_amount) AS max_order_total FROM orders;

question: What is the average order value by state?
expected_sql: SELECT c.state, AVG(o.total_amount) AS avg_order_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.state ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC;

question: How many distinct products were sold in 2023?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT oi.product_id) AS distinct_products FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE strftime('%Y', o.order_date) = '2023';

question: What is total revenue per month in 2024?
expected_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', order_date) AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE strftime('%Y', order_date) = '2024' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month;

question: Which product category generates the most orders?
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id) AS order_count FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 1;

question: What is the average number of items per order?
expected_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS avg_items_per_order FROM order_items;

question: List the 5 cheapest products.
expected_sql: SELECT product_name, price FROM products ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 5;

question: How much revenue came from customers in California?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE c.state = 'CA';

question: What is the total number of orders?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_orders FROM orders;

question: Which month had the highest revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', order_date) AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders GROUP BY month ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 1;

question: What is the average price of products in the Books category?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(price) AS avg_price FROM products WHERE category = 'Books';

question: How many orders had a total over 500 dollars?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS large_orders FROM orders WHERE total_amount > 500;

question: What is the total lifetime revenue across all orders?
expected_sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders;

question: Which customers spent more than 2000 dollars in total?
expected_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_spend FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id GROUP BY c.customer_id HAVING total_spend > 2000 ORDER BY total_spend DESC;

question: What is the number of orders per month over time?
expected_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', order_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY month ORDER BY month;

question: What is the average order total for orders placed in December?
expected_sql: SELECT AVG(total_amount) AS avg_total FROM orders WHERE strftime('%m', order_date) = '12';

question: How many products cost more than 100 dollars?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS expensive_products FROM products WHERE price > 100;

question: What are the top 3 states by total revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT c.state, SUM(o.total_amount) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id GROUP BY c.state ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 3;

question: How many unique customers placed at least one order?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS active_customers FROM orders;
